summary: Iteration 11 — 4-pillar review: Lead Mgmt live, Install Schedule alerts, Email Activity hardening, Payment Gateway key validation. 25/27 PASS (1 SKIP, 2 FAIL). All UI test-ids and admin page renders are clean. Two real backend bugs identified: (a) /ajax/notify-stock.php DOES NOT actually run email_address_deliverable() because mailer.php is never require_once'd before the function_exists() guard, so the new validation block is dead code on that endpoint; (b) ensure_admin() in /app/php-version/includes/functions.php is a misnamed SEED function (only ensures the admin row exists in the DB) — it does NOT enforce auth — therefore /ajax/leads-online.php AND /ajax/validate-gateway-key.php are reachable by unauthenticated callers and leak customer lead PII / allow free Stripe + PayPal ping abuse.

critical.issue: ensure_admin() (line 25) does NOT enforce admin auth — it only seeds the admin row in the users table. Unauthenticated curl returns full JSON {ok:true, online_ids, total, install_pending, latest:[{id,name,email,product,created_at},...]} — leaks customer PII (names + emails) of every chat lead. Should call require_admin() instead.

Same auth bug: ensure_admin() does not enforce auth. Any anonymous internet caller can POST and the server will make outbound calls to api.stripe.com and api-m.sandbox.paypal.com on demand. Abuse / SSRF-lite vector. Fix: replace ensure_admin() with require_admin().

Email deliverability validation is dead code. Line 27 checks `function_exists('email_address_deliverable')` but the file is never loaded (no `require_once __DIR__ . '/../includes/mailer.php'`). Bogus email foo@no-such-domain-xyzz123.con accepted with HTTP 200 ok:true + insert into stock_notifications. Pillar #3 promise (real failure, not silent success) is NOT honoured on this endpoint. Fix: add `require_once __DIR__ . '/../includes/mailer.php';` above the function_exists guard, mirroring what /ajax/lead.php line 26 already does correctly.

minor.issue: When ensure_admin() is fixed to actually authenticate, the public_session test (test_leads_online_requires_admin) will pass automatically — currently it FAILS with 200 because the endpoint responds to anonymous callers.

action_items: CRITICAL — replace `ensure_admin()` with `require_admin()` in /app/php-version/ajax/leads-online.php (line 25) AND /app/php-version/ajax/validate-gateway-key.php (line 21). Current `ensure_admin()` only seeds the admin row in the DB; it does not authenticate. Anonymous callers currently get customer PII and can abuse the Stripe/PayPal ping.

HIGH — in /app/php-version/ajax/notify-stock.php, add `require_once __DIR__ . '/../includes/mailer.php';` directly above line 27 (the function_exists check). Currently the function is never loaded, so the validation block silently skips and bogus emails are accepted with 200 ok:true.
Optional rename of ensure_admin() → seed_admin_user() for clarity, then grep the codebase for every caller and convert them to require_admin() where actual auth was intended.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/php-version/includes/functions.php:391 — function ensure_admin() is the world's most dangerous misnomer. It SEEDS the admin row instead of REQUIRING admin auth. Any endpoint that calls it as a gate (leads-online, validate-gateway-key, possibly others) is wide open. Grep '/app/php-version' for `ensure_admin\(\)` and audit every caller.

/app/php-version/ajax/notify-stock.php:27 — function_exists() guard without a require_once for the file that defines the function is a foot-gun. Either explicitly require mailer.php or drop the guard entirely (mailer.php should already be loaded by the project bootstrap).

/app/php-version/admin.php is now 10,552 lines — still well above the 700-line guideline. Same call-out as iteration 10 to extract install-schedule + api-validate handler blocks into separate includes/.

Stripe + PayPal prefix short-circuit (sk_live_ / sk_test_ / rk_) is solid; both real-API rejections come back with HTTP 401 + clear human-readable error messages — pillar #4 promise is delivered correctly at the endpoint level. The only remaining gap is auth.

success_rate.backend: 25/27 PASS (92.6%) + 1 skip (indexnow key file not in robots.txt — non-blocking)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — every required data-testid asserted via server-side HTML scrape (install-pending-banner, adm-nav-leads-badge, adm-nav-schedule-badge, api-card-validate-test/live, api-card-result-test/live, api-paypal-validate-test/live, api-paypal-result-test/live). No Playwright UI smoke ran because every UI surface under review is server-rendered and the assertions were exhaustive in HTML.

seed_data_creation: Tests inserted 1 chat_lead row (name='TEST_valid', email='valid@gmail.com') and likely 1 stock_notifications row (slug from /shop.php discovery + email='foo@no-such-domain-xyzz123.con'). The bad-email row is itself evidence of the notify-stock bug (it should never have been inserted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file lives at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_email_validation_and_gateway.py — re-run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_email_validation_and_gateway.py -v` once the main agent ships fixes for (a) ensure_admin→require_admin in leads-online + validate-gateway-key and (b) the missing require_once mailer.php in notify-stock. The 2 currently-failing tests are TestEmailValidationNotifyStock::test_notify_stock_no_mx_rejected and TestLeadsOnline::test_leads_online_requires_admin — both should flip to PASS after fixes. The dev-mode email_outbox check passed via admin Email Activity HTML scrape (substring 'Pending delivery' / 'configure SMTP' / 'queued' detected).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause #1 (leads-online + validate-gateway-key auth bypass): /app/php-version/includes/functions.php defines TWO superficially-similar helpers — ensure_admin() at line 391 (which is purely a seed function that inserts the admin row when missing) and require_admin() at line 409 (which actually enforces $_SESSION['user_id'] + role==='admin'). The new endpoints called ensure_admin() thinking it was the auth gate. It isn't. Repro: `curl https://indexnow-checker.preview.emergentagent.com/ajax/leads-online.php` from anywhere on the internet returns full lead list with names + emails. Mitigation: change line 25 of leads-online.php and line 21 of validate-gateway-key.php from `ensure_admin();` to `require_admin();`. Root cause #2 (notify-stock dead validation): the function_exists() guard succeeds only if the file containing email_address_deliverable() has already been included in this PHP request. lead.php loads mailer.php explicitly at line 26 BEFORE the guard; notify-stock.php does not — so the function is never defined when notify-stock runs in isolation, function_exists returns false, the if-block is skipped, and the bogus email proceeds to insert + send_email. Repro: `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"product_slug":"<any-real-slug>","email":"foo@no-such-domain-xyzz123.con"}' https://indexnow-checker.preview.emergentagent.com/ajax/notify-stock.php` returns HTTP 200 ok:true.
